gate_approach: MSCSIA selected as gate guide — only cs_grad guide with a Capstone section; tests the most structurally varied format in the family. All 5 guides parsed in the same pass.

guides.degree_title: Master of Science, Cybersecurity and Information Assurance
guides.note: Unique 1-course-per-group structure: each of the 9 AoS groups contains exactly 1 course. Parser handles this correctly — group and course emit in the standard 2-item pending_titles resolution. Capstone section present with 1 bullet.

guides.degree_title: Master of Science, Computer Science, Computing Systems
Master of Science, Computer Science, Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning
guides.note: Degree title in parsed output is truncated ('...Artificial Intelligence and') due to multi-line header in source. Full title is 'Master of Science, Computer Science, Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning'. Cosmetic gap — metadata, SP, and AoS content are all correct.

guides.degree_title: Master of Science, Computer Science, Human-Computer Interaction
Bachelor of Science, Computer Science (BSCS to MSCS)
guides.reconciliation: FAILED — 1 SP title not in AoS, 8 AoS titles not in SP
guides.low_detail: SP table has column ordering issues typical of the BSITM/MATSPED failure class. Invalid CU/Term values observed (e.g., 'Discrete Mathematics I' parsed as CUs=44, Term=33; 'Business of IT - Applications' CUs=4, Term=87). 8 sp_expected_term_got_text anomalies; 4 sp_row_invalid anomalies; 1 sp_incomplete_row_at_eof. This is a source-PDF artifact — same failure class as BSITM and MATSPED. AoS content is intact: 11 groups, 37 courses, 0 AoS anomalies, 0 empty descriptions, 0 empty competency lists. The guide covers the full BSCS-to-MSCS bridge curriculum (37 courses including both undergraduate and graduate content).

structural_notes: MSCSIA uses a 1-course-per-group structure (9 groups, 9 courses). Structurally valid; parser handles it cleanly.
MSCSIA is the only cs_grad guide with an explicit Capstone section. Capstone has 1 bullet.
MSCSCS, MSCSAIML, MSCSHCI are standard 10-course graduate MS guides with 2–4 AoS groups. No capstone.

MSCSAIML degree title is truncated in the parsed header metadata ('...Artificial Intelligence and'). This is a header-line wrapping artifact — the full program name is longer than the single-line extraction. Not a parser bug; cosmetic only.

MSCSUG is a BSCS-to-MSCS bridge/pathway guide covering the full 37-course undergraduate+graduate curriculum. SP table has column extraction failure (same class as BSITM, MATSPED). AoS content (37 courses, 11 groups) is fully intact.
The 4 pure MSCS guides (MSCSIA, MSCSCS, MSCSAIML, MSCSHCI) share consistent header-line metadata and 3-column multiline SP format.
All 4 pure MSCS guides are version 202504 (MSCSCS, MSCSAIML, MSCSHCI) or 202306 (MSCSIA).
No new parser logic needed or applied for any guide in this family.
gate_result: PASS — 4/5 guides HIGH confidence. 1 LOW (MSCSUG) is a source-artifact bridge guide with SP column extraction failure, same class as BSITM and MATSPED. AoS for MSCSUG is intact.

recommendation.summary: 4/5 guides HIGH confidence. MSCSUG is LOW due to SP extraction failure (source artifact). Roll out family with MSCSUG SP data excluded from downstream use. AoS content for all 5 guides is usable.
recommendation.mscsug_disposition: Source-artifact outlier. SP data unusable. AoS data (37 courses, 11 groups, 0 anomalies) is intact and usable. Same handling as BSITM and MATSPED.
